_note: Reviewer follow-up #1 (round 5): when a wrapper has BOTH `<Inner on:click />` (component bubble) AND `<button on:click>` (DOM bubble) for the SAME event name, pre-fix the two were built into separate type fragments and INTERSECTED — neither shape was a usable consumer-handler arg. Upstream svelte2tsx runs all bubbles through one `EventHandler.bubbledEvents` map; DOM sources call `Map.set(name, expr)` which REPLACES the entire entry, component sources call `set(name, [].concat(exist, exp))` which APPENDS to whatever is there (DOM or component). Post-fix native merges DOM and component bubbles into ONE position-ordered list per name: DOM REPLACES the list, Component APPENDS. This fixture exercises the component-then-DOM direction (Inner → button) — the later DOM source REPLACES the component entry, so `click` resolves to `HTMLElementEventMap['click']` (MouseEvent) only. The consumer's MouseEvent handler passes; the CustomEvent handler typed for Inner's payload now correctly fails TS2345. (See fixture 120 for the opposite-direction union case.)
